title: Water Heating Timeout
shortDescription: The water has not reached the target temperature within the allotted time.
fullDescription: Error E01 on Thermador dishwashers (BSH platform) indicates the water temperature did not reach the target within the allowed heating time. The heating element, temperature sensor, or water supply temperature may be the cause.

causes.cause: Incoming water temperature is too low

fixSteps.title: Check hot water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Run the kitchen faucet until hot before starting the dishwasher. Incoming water should be at least 120F.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Access from below. Measure resistance: 10-25 ohms. OL means burned out.

fixSteps.title: Test temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Measure sensor resistance. Compare to BSH specifications for your model.

fixSteps.title: Replace the heater
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and water. Remove old heater and install replacement.

whenToCallPro: If heater and sensor are fine, the board relay needs diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E01 water heating error fix

faq.q: Is the Thermador dishwasher the same as Bosch?
faq.a: Yes. Thermador dishwashers are built on the BSH (Bosch) platform and share many components and error codes.

faq.q: Should I run hot water before starting?
faq.a: Yes. Running the faucet until hot ensures the dishwasher fills with hot water from the start.

faq.q: Can hard water affect heating?
faq.a: Scale buildup on the heater from hard water reduces heating efficiency over time.

title: Temperature Sensor Failure
shortDescription: The water temperature sensor has failed or is out of range.
fullDescription: Error E02 indicates the NTC temperature sensor is open, shorted, or reading outside the expected range. The dishwasher cannot regulate water heating.

fixSteps.title: Access the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The sensor is near the sump/heater area. Access from below by removing the kick plate.

fixSteps.title: Test resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and measure. At room temp approximately 50K ohms. At 120F approximately 12K ohms.

fixSteps.title: Check connections
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure connector is firm and clean.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove old sensor and install replacement.

whenToCallPro: If new sensor does not fix E02.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E02 temperature sensor error fix

faq.q: Is this the same sensor as Bosch?
faq.a: Yes, same BSH part number.

faq.q: Can the dishwasher run with E02?
faq.a: It may run but cannot heat water properly.

faq.q: How do I access the sensor?
faq.a: Remove the kick plate and access from below the tub.

title: Water Heating Element Open Circuit
shortDescription: The heating element circuit is open — the element has burned out.
fullDescription: Error E09 specifically indicates the heating element has an open circuit (burned out). Unlike E01 which is a timeout, E09 detects that the element itself is not conducting.

fixSteps.title: Test the heater
fixSteps.instruction: Access from below. Measure resistance: 10-25 ohms. OL confirms burned out.

fixSteps.title: Check connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect heater wiring and connectors for damage.

fixSteps.title: Check for leaks
fixSteps.instruction: Water leaking onto the heater can cause premature failure. Inspect the tub and hoses.

fixSteps.title: Replace the heater
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and water. Remove old heater from sump, install replacement.

whenToCallPro: If heater tests good but E09 persists.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E09 heating element open fix

faq.q: Is E09 the same as E01?
faq.a: No. E01 is a heating timeout. E09 specifically means the element is burned out.

faq.q: How long do dishwasher heaters last?
faq.a: 8-12 years typically.

faq.q: Can hard water shorten heater life?
faq.a: Yes. Scale buildup insulates the heater, causing it to overheat and fail prematurely.

title: Water Flow Sensor Error
shortDescription: The water flow sensor is not detecting proper water flow.
fullDescription: Error E14 indicates the flow sensor (flow meter) is not detecting water entering the dishwasher. The sensor may have failed, or no water is reaching the dishwasher.

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure shutoff valve under the sink is fully open.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Access the valve. Test solenoid resistance (200-500 ohms).

fixSteps.title: Test the flow sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The flow sensor is in the water inlet path. Check for debris blocking the impeller.

fixSteps.title: Replace flow sensor or valve
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the faulty component.

whenToCallPro: If supply is good and both components test fine.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E14 flow sensor error fix

faq.q: What is a flow sensor?
faq.a: A small turbine that measures water flow rate entering the dishwasher.

faq.q: Can debris block the flow sensor?
faq.a: Yes. Small particles can jam the flow sensor impeller.

faq.q: Is the flow sensor shared with Bosch?
faq.a: Yes, same BSH component.

title: Water in Base Pan — Leak Protection Activated
shortDescription: Water has been detected in the base pan, activating the anti-flood system.
fullDescription: Error E15 is the BSH anti-flood protection code. A float switch in the base pan has detected accumulated water. The dishwasher shuts down to prevent water damage. This is one of the most common Thermador/Bosch dishwasher errors.

fixSteps.title: Tilt the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the dishwasher. Carefully tilt it back about 45 degrees to drain water off the float switch. Place towels underneath.

fixSteps.title: Drain the base pan
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the kick plate. Use towels or a wet-dry vacuum to remove water from the base pan.

fixSteps.title: Find the leak source
fixSteps.instruction: Run a short cycle and watch underneath for the leak source. Common locations: door seal, hose connections, sump gaskets.

fixSteps.title: Fix the leak
fixSteps.instruction: Tighten loose hose connections, replace damaged seals, or repair the sump gasket.

whenToCallPro: If you cannot identify the leak source or if the sump is cracked.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E15 water in base pan leak fix

faq.q: Is E15 common?
faq.a: Yes. E15 is one of the most frequently reported errors on BSH (Bosch/Thermador/Siemens) dishwashers.

faq.q: Why does the drain pump run continuously?
faq.a: The control activates the drain pump when water is detected in the base to try to remove it.

faq.q: Will tilting fix E15 permanently?
faq.a: No. Tilting clears the float switch temporarily. You must find and fix the leak source.

title: Filter or Drain Blockage
shortDescription: The dishwasher drain system is blocked, preventing water removal.
fullDescription: Error E22 indicates the dishwasher cannot drain properly. The filters may be clogged, the drain pump may be blocked, or the drain hose has an obstruction.

causes.cause: Filters are clogged with food debris
Drain hose is kinked or clogged

fixSteps.title: Clean the filters
fixSteps.instruction: Remove lower rack, spray arm, and filter assembly. Clean thoroughly under running water.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect for kinks. If connected to a disposal, run the disposal. Check knockout plug.

fixSteps.title: Inspect drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Access from below. Check impeller for debris. Ensure it spins freely.

fixSteps.title: Test drain pump motor
fixSteps.instruction: If pump does not run, test resistance. Replace if failed.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning does not resolve E22 and the pump has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E22 drain blockage error fix

faq.q: How often should I clean the filter?
faq.a: Thermador recommends cleaning the filter after every few cycles, or at minimum once per month.

faq.q: What is the knockout plug?
faq.a: A plastic plug in new garbage disposals that must be knocked out for the dishwasher drain connection.

faq.q: Can I manually drain the dishwasher?
faq.a: Yes. Bail out water with a cup and soak up the rest with towels.

title: Drain Pump Error
shortDescription: The drain pump is not removing water within the expected time.
fullDescription: Error E24 indicates the dishwasher detected water remaining after the drain cycle timed out. This is similar to E22 but specifically indicates a drain timeout rather than just a blockage detection.

causes.cause: Air gap or high loop in drain hose missing

symptoms: Drain pump runs but water stays
Drain pump does not run at all

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose routing
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the drain hose has a high loop under the counter or connects through an air gap. Without this, water can siphon back.

fixSteps.title: Clean filters and sump
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean all filters. Check the sump for debris.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose and disposal
fixSteps.instruction: Run the disposal to clear it. Check hose for kinks.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If pump does not run, test motor resistance and replace if failed.

whenToCallPro: If all drain paths are clear and the pump has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E24 drain pump error fix

faq.q: What is a drain high loop?
faq.a: A loop in the drain hose that goes up to the underside of the counter before going down to the disposal or drain. Prevents backflow.

faq.q: Is E24 the same as E22?
faq.a: Similar. E22 is a general drain blockage. E24 is specifically a drain timeout.

faq.q: Should the drain hose connect to the disposal or drain pipe?
faq.a: Either is acceptable. Connection to a disposal is most common but a direct drain connection with proper air gap also works.

title: Drain Pump Impeller Blocked
shortDescription: The drain pump cover or impeller area has a blockage.
fullDescription: Error E25 is specific to a physical blockage at the drain pump cover or impeller. Unlike E22/E24 which may indicate hose issues, E25 points specifically to the pump area.

causes.cause: Debris blocking the drain pump impeller
Drain pump cover is not properly seated
Broken glass or bones in the pump

symptoms: Pump hums but does not drain

fixSteps.title: Remove the drain pump cover
fixSteps.instruction: Inside the tub, remove the fine filter and coarse filter. Locate the drain pump cover (usually a small round cap near the filter). Twist to remove.

fixSteps.title: Clear the impeller
fixSteps.instruction: Check the pump impeller for debris. Remove any glass, bones, or foreign objects.

fixSteps.title: Check impeller rotation
fixSteps.instruction: The impeller should spin freely. If it is stuck even after clearing debris, it may be damaged.

fixSteps.title: Reseat the pump cover
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the pump cover, making sure it clicks into the locked position. Reinstall filters.

whenToCallPro: If the impeller is physically damaged and the pump needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E25 drain pump impeller blocked fix

faq.q: How do I prevent E25?
faq.a: Scrape all food off dishes before loading. Avoid putting items with bones or broken glass in the dishwasher.

faq.q: Is the pump cover easy to remove?
faq.a: Yes. It twists off without tools on most Thermador/BSH dishwashers.

faq.q: Can broken glass damage the pump?
faq.a: Yes. Small glass shards can jam and damage the impeller, requiring pump replacement.

title: Power Supply Voltage Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher is detecting incorrect supply voltage.
fullDescription: Error E27 indicates the control board has detected that the supply voltage is outside the acceptable range. Thermador dishwashers require a dedicated 120V 15A circuit.

causes.cause: Low voltage from shared circuit or long wire run
Loose connection at the junction box
Control board voltage sensing circuit failed

symptoms: Error during heavy home electrical use

fixSteps.title: Check outlet voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Measure voltage at the dishwasher junction box. Should be 120V (+/- 10%).

fixSteps.title: Check circuit dedication
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the dishwasher is on its own dedicated 15A circuit, not shared with other appliances.

fixSteps.title: Tighten connections
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker. Check and tighten all wire connections at the junction box.

fixSteps.title: Contact an electrician
fixSteps.instruction: If voltage is consistently low, the circuit may need upgrading.

whenToCallPro: If voltage is low, call an electrician.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E27 power voltage error fix

faq.q: Does the dishwasher need its own circuit?
faq.a: Yes. A dedicated 120V 15A circuit is required.

faq.q: Can an extension cord cause E27?
faq.a: Dishwashers should never be on an extension cord. Direct wiring or a proper outlet is required.

faq.q: Will a surge protector help?
faq.a: A whole-house surge protector can help, but E27 is about voltage level, not surges.

title: Water Fill Timeout
shortDescription: The dishwasher did not fill with water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error E04 indicates the dishwasher did not detect sufficient water level within the fill time limit. The water supply may be restricted or the inlet valve may have failed.

causes.cause: Water supply valve is closed or restricted
Float switch is stuck in the up position

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the shutoff valve under the sink is fully open.

fixSteps.title: Check supply line
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect for kinks or blockages. A kinked copper or braided line restricts flow.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Access from below. Test solenoid resistance (200-500 ohms).

fixSteps.title: Replace inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water. Disconnect and replace the valve.

whenToCallPro: If supply is fine and valve tests good.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E04 water fill timeout error fix

faq.q: What is the minimum water pressure?
faq.a: 20 psi minimum for proper valve operation.

faq.q: Can a clogged supply screen cause E04?
faq.a: Yes. The inlet valve has a small screen that can clog with mineral deposits.

faq.q: Is E04 related to E14?
faq.a: Both are water supply issues. E14 is specifically the flow sensor. E04 is a general fill timeout.

title: Door Switch or Latch Error
shortDescription: The door switch does not confirm the door is securely closed.
fullDescription: Error E06 indicates the door latch switch is not registering a secure door closure. The dishwasher will not start or will stop mid-cycle for safety.

causes.cause: Wiring to door switch is damaged

symptoms: Control panel active but no cycle

fixSteps.title: Close door firmly
fixSteps.instruction: Push door firmly and listen for a click. Check for obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Inspect latch
fixSteps.instruction: Check the latch mechanism for wear or damage.

fixSteps.title: Test door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Test continuity when door is latched.

fixSteps.title: Replace latch/switch
fixSteps.instruction: Remove old component and install replacement.

whenToCallPro: If latch and switch are fine but E06 persists.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E06 door latch switch error fix

faq.q: Can I bypass the door switch?
faq.a: Never. It prevents flooding by ensuring the dishwasher only runs with the door closed.

faq.q: Why does the latch wear out?
faq.a: Daily opening and closing wears the plastic mechanism over 5-10 years.

faq.q: Is the latch the same as Bosch?
faq.a: Yes, same BSH platform component.

title: Diverter Valve Error
shortDescription: The water diverter valve is not functioning correctly.
fullDescription: Error E19 indicates the diverter valve motor that switches water between spray arms is not operating. This results in only partial spray arm coverage during the wash cycle.
symptoms: Top or bottom rack dishes not clean

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker for 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Clean sump area
fixSteps.instruction: Remove filters and clean thoroughly. Debris can jam the diverter.

fixSteps.title: Access diverter motor
fixSteps.instruction: From underneath, locate the diverter on the sump assembly. Test operation.

fixSteps.title: Replace diverter motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and water. Remove and replace the motor.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning and motor replacement do not resolve E19.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador dishwasher E19 diverter valve error fix

faq.q: What does the diverter do?
faq.a: It alternates water flow between upper and lower spray arms for full rack coverage.

faq.q: Can I run the dishwasher with E19?
faq.a: It will run but only partially clean since one spray zone will not receive water.

faq.q: Is the Thermador diverter the same as Bosch?
faq.a: Yes, same BSH component.
